evidence: The codebase is highly compliant with idiomatic Rust naming (snake_case) and organization standards.
A minor outlier is the placement of ui.rs at the root while the app layer has been moved to a src/app/ sub-package. This creates an inconsistent level of nesting for key modules.
The use of .ok() for swallowing errors in send_event (src/app/types.rs) is a minor style island compared to theResult-heavy pattern found elsewhere.

issues.summary: UI module is at the root while newer app modules are nested
evidence: ui.rs lives in src/ despite being a domain package similar to the ones now living in src/app/.
issues.suggestion: Move ui.rs to src/ui/mod.rs to maintain a consistent organizational hierarchy.

root_causes: Partial application of the new modularization strategy.
likely_symptoms: Mildly confusing import paths (e.g., crate::ui vs crate::app::state).
